figures.caption: Figure 1: Left: Autoregressive models exhibit abrupt representation changes, causing temporal inconsistencies. Right: Our ReCo enforces smooth transitions via continuity loss, yielding temporally consistent videos.

Figure 8: Loss distribution of different optimization strategies on training and generated samples. (Top) Comparison of loss distributions between “Local-Opt.” on its generated samples (“Local-Opt. on Gen.”) and its training samples (“LocalOpt. on Train”). (Bottom) Comparison of loss distributions for “Local-Opt.” and “Local-Opt. w/ first frame”, where the first frame of the video is provided as ground truth, on generated samples. The red dashed lines indicate frame blocks corresponding to the spatiotemporal compression of VQVAE.
Figure 12: More Error Cases from Fewer-Frames model.
Figure 10: Loss distribution comparison on generated samples. (Top) “Baseline” vs. “Local-Opt. w/ balanced.” (Bottom) “Baseline” vs. “Ours”.
